input: Oh fantastic, it died the moment the warranty expired. Impeccable timing.
Sure, who doesn't love waiting two hours for a table they reserved weeks ago?
Wow, a charger that stops working in three days. Truly cutting-edge engineering.
Great, another update that removed the one feature I actually used. Bravo.
Amazing, the 'waterproof' jacket soaked through in a light drizzle. Shocking.
Yeah, nothing says luxury hotel like a cold shower and a broken elevator.
Love how the 'noise-cancelling' headphones cancel exactly zero noise. Genius.
Perfect, the screen cracked in my pocket. Gorilla Glass, my foot.
Oh joy, they delivered it to the wrong address for the third time. Consistent.
Brilliant, a laptop that overheats if you dare to open two browser tabs.
Honestly? I braced for junk and this thing is embarrassingly good. Take my money.
Ugh, fine, I'll admit it: best purchase I've made all year. Don't tell my wallet.
Great, now I have to return it because it works too well and shames my old one.
Oh no, a vacuum that actually picks up dirt. How will I ever cope.
Terrible, truly awful, that I now have to recommend this to literally everyone.
Wonderful, an airline that lost my bag and then found new ways to not care.
Fantastic customer service, if your goal is to be hung up on four times.
Oh sure, 'all-day battery' — if your day happens to end at 11am.
Loved paying premium prices for a hotel room with a view of a brick wall.
What a treat, the app crashes every time I try to actually save anything.
Incredible, I'm genuinely annoyed at how much I love this stupid little gadget.
Oh great, the 'quiet' dishwasher that sounds like a jet taking off. Lovely.
Well played, chef — a steak so overcooked I could resole my shoes with it.
Sure, buy the extended warranty, it'll be just as useless as the product.
Fine, you win, this ugly little coffee maker makes better coffee than my old fancy one.
Oh wonderful, a 'smart' TV too dumb to remember the Wi-Fi password overnight.
How lovely, the assembly instructions were clearly written by someone who hates me.
I hate to say it — really, I do — but this thing is annoyingly perfect.
Marvelous, the 'stain-resistant' couch stained the first time someone looked at it.
Oh good, the printer's out of ink again, right on cue for my deadline. Reliable.
Shockingly, the budget earbuds I bought as a joke sound better than my expensive ones.
Great, the 'one-size-fits-all' shirt fits absolutely no one on this planet.
Ugh, now I'm that person who won't shut up about a blender. It's that good.
Perfect, a hotel that charges for Wi-Fi so slow you could fax faster.
Oh delightful, the wheels fell off the 'premium' suitcase on its maiden trip.
Wow, an actually comfortable office chair. I'm devastated I have no complaints.
Lovely, the 'fresh' salad came with a side of someone else's hair. Five stars.
Sure, nothing beats a fitness tracker that thinks sleeping is 12,000 steps.
Terribly inconvenient, really, how this thing just refuses to break no matter what.
Oh brilliant, tech support whose only advice is to turn it off and give up.
